user_prompt: Draw a coordinate plane with a circle centered at point O = (6, −5) with radius 10. Draw two perpendicular radii OA and OB — let OA go straight up to A = (6, 5). Then I know there's a point C on the circle whose x-coordinate is 12. Draw a vertical dashed line at x = 12 that intersects the circle at point C. Draw the right triangle from O to a point D = (12, −5) directly to the right of O, and then up to C. Label O, A, B, C, D, mark the right angle at D, and show the legs OD = 6 and DC = 8 so I can verify OC = 10.
